title: Roupala pinnata
intro: Roupala pinnata is a species of plant in the family Cunoniaceae, commonly known as the "Roupala tree." This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Roupala pinnata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Roupala pinnata is commonly used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory tract infections and to promote general well-being.
taxonomy: Roupala pinnata is classified within the plant family Cunoniaceae and the order Oxalidales. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species is further categorized as a member of the genus Roupala, which is native to the Americas. Roupala pinnata is a species of flowering plant known for its unique features and distribution. This classification places Roupala pinnata within the broader context of plant taxonomy.
uses: Roupala pinnata is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, where it is employed to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory disorders. In addition, Roupala pinnata is thought to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which could contribute to its potential therapeutic applications. Some studies suggest that the plant may also have antiviral and antiparasitic properties, although further research is needed to confirm these findings. However, more extensive research is required to fully understand the medicinal potential of Roupala pinnata and to establish its safety and efficacy in human use.

parts: Roupala pinnata is a plant species native to South America, and its medicinal parts include the leaves and bark, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bark is also used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The roots of Roupala pinnata are sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma.
